codeReview.summary: Reviewing the original graph-shell diff, the later regression that reintroduced `#proof`, and this fix diff shows the remaining hero-CTA blocker is now resolved. `src/app/page.tsx` again points the primary hero CTA at `#graph-proof`, existing expectations were updated, and the new regression file covers the hero CTA, shared navigation destinations, proof-section in-page CTAs, and proof-node hash parsing so a prominent legacy anchor-scroll route cannot quietly return through the main shell. I did not find any remaining blocking or non-blocking issues in the reviewed fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The milestone's recorded blocker attribution is stale: the original `graph-shell-navigation-and-node-first-exploration` commit already changed the hero CTA to `#graph-proof`, and the legacy `#proof` destination was reintroduced later by `graph-shell-cross-surface-handoffs-and-context` before this fix restored it. Shared review state should distinguish the original reviewed feature from a later stacked regression so blocker ownership stays traceable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `git show 29b15a4305a8435c73c03b770a23358bfba54522 -- src/app/page.tsx` changes the primary CTA from `#live-now` to `#graph-proof`; `git show b532c10a4bf2c5280f9af4c679a7ab6243df9b63 -- src/app/page.tsx` changes it from `#graph-proof` back to `#proof`; the prior review at `.factory/validation/graph-first-shell-and-token-policy/scrutiny/reviews/graph-shell-navigation-and-node-first-exploration.json` and synthesis file still attribute the blocker to `graph-shell-navigation-and-node-first-exploration`.

summary: Pass. The fix restores the hero primary CTA to the proof graph focus-state path, updates the affected expectations, and adds focused regression coverage that guards hero and other prominent in-page CTA destinations against reverting to legacy section anchors. One synthesis-worthy observation remains: the original blocker appears to have been introduced by a later stacked commit, so the current scrutiny state over-attributes that regression to the earlier graph-shell feature.
